<files>

packages/rex/tests/e2e/fixtures/sample-prd/.rex/config.json (JSON, 6 lines, test)
packages/rex/tests/e2e/fixtures/sample-prd/.rex/execution-log.jsonl (Other, 3 lines, test)
packages/rex/tests/e2e/fixtures/sample-prd/.rex/prd.json (JSON, 95 lines, test)
packages/rex/tests/e2e/fixtures/sample-prd/.rex/workflow.md (Markdown, 18 lines, test)

<findings>

[observation] [info] High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
[observation] [info] Consider adding a fixture validation step in CI that asserts the sample-prd fixture passes rex validate, ensuring fixture data stays schema-compatible as the PRD schema evolves.
[observation] [info] Perfect cohesion (1.0) and zero coupling for a pure data zone is correct — these fixture files are consumed by the filesystem, not the module graph, so import metrics are not meaningful here.
[observation] [info] The fixture PRD state should be kept intentionally minimal and stable; avoid adding real operational data from development runs as that would couple e2e tests to evolving project state.
[suggestion] [info] The fixture execution-log.jsonl can silently drift from the production log schema because only the live log is exercised by hench. Add a schema validation step in the e2e test harness that parses the fixture log against the same schema as production to catch format drift before it surfaces as a test failure.

</findings>

<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- All four files are inert JSON/markdown data with no imports (cohesion 1, coupling 0) — this zone exists purely to seed a realistic .rex/ directory for e2e test scenarios.
- No entry points means these fixtures are consumed by test runners via filesystem reads, not module imports — the zone boundary is correct and the isolation is clean.
- Committing fixture prd.json and execution-log.jsonl to source control is intentional here (unlike the dogfooding .rex/ in the package root) because fixtures must be deterministic and version-controlled for reproducible e2e runs.
- Perfect cohesion (1.0) and zero coupling for a pure data zone is correct — these fixture files are consumed by the filesystem, not the module graph, so import metrics are not meaningful here.
- The fixture PRD state should be kept intentionally minimal and stable; avoid adding real operational data from development runs as that would couple e2e tests to evolving project state.
- Consider adding a fixture validation step in CI that asserts the sample-prd fixture passes rex validate, ensuring fixture data stays schema-compatible as the PRD schema evolves.
- The fixture execution-log.jsonl mirrors the format of the live .rex/execution-log.jsonl. If the execution log schema gains new required fields, hench validates only the live log — the fixture will silently diverge and may cause deserialization failures in e2e tests that exercise log-reading paths.
- The fixture execution-log.jsonl can silently drift from the production log schema because only the live log is exercised by hench. Add a schema validation step in the e2e test harness that parses the fixture log against the same schema as production to catch format drift before it surfaces as a test failure.

</insights>
